paragraph: The single-family market in Moonachie is extremely sparse, with four sales over eight months at a median of $752,500. That thin volume makes month-to-month comparisons meaningless, but the data here does point to a buyer-friendly environment. The median sale-to-list ratio is 0.95, meaning homes are selling below asking on average, and the median days on market sits at 21.5 days. One quarter of the homes sold went for asking or above, which tells you that properly priced homes will still find a buyer, but sellers don't have the leverage to overprice. If you're looking in Moonachie, you'll need patience to find inventory, but when you do, you're negotiating from a position where pricing competitively matters. The range of sales from $585,000 to $780,000 shows there's genuine variety in what's available when homes do come to market.
